turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: But one of the things that -- that bothers me here is that if -- if the Court issues a subpoena duces tecum to -- to a corporation and the corporation chooses anyone at once to respond to the -- to the subpoena, it chooses the vice president of the -- of the company and he doesn't produce the records and he's committed for contempt, should it be open to him or to the corporation to say, “Well, we didn't -- we didn't have the proper person return this. You can't hold this man accountable because he really didn't -- he really didn't know anything about what we should have done was to have our -- our controller or head bookkeeper or somebody else who knows the record to this but this man didn't so -- so there's no way of enforcing a subpoena.”

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: Well, I wouldn't say there's no way of enforcing it, Your Honor. They did secure through the impounding order but more directly to your question under the unique circumstances of this case were here, all the responsible officers were tied up by the pendency of this criminal trial and where this individual who is described by the trial judge himself as merely a nominal officer, a nominal vice president of this company whose duties were so illegal in nature, where he voluntary attempts to comply. It seems to me that you should not hold him to the high uncompromising standard that a responsible officer, let's say the president or the Secretary of Treasure would have in responding to that, that you will recognize a good faith effort in this -- on this petitioner's part where you might not say that was enough on the part of a person who is primarily responsible for the -- for those records and the keeping of those records.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: May I take into account the totality of the situation that this was a lawyer?

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: Well, Your Honor, that raises the question as to how much of a lawyer he was. He was --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: How can we decide that here?

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: Not -- certainty, he was not a lawyer in relation to this criminal case.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: You said he at one -- the record shows that he said he's a good enough lawyer, not to need any lawyer to defend him.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: And it's also -- it -- it seems to me impossible as far the sentence is concerned to -- and the fact that he was a lawyer to dissociate the fact that what really concerned the trial judge apparently and what obviously concerned the Court of Appeals was the first specification.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: I'm with you on that.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: On -- and I think that's reflected in the sentencing and -- and I think that the fact that he was a lawyer, he's probably primary in that.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: I'm with you on the sentencing part.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: And the fact that he was a lawyer, I don't think distinguishes him here though insofar as his obedience or his attempt to make the good faith search.

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: But it bears on all these things of what he knows, what's its citation and whether he has time and all of the business that makes difference between a lawyer and a layman who comes into court even he's very bright and distinguished, he had to be (Inaudible) by court proceedings.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: But he was pretty closely connected with this whole subject matter, wasn't he, he was an original defendant in a criminal conspiracy that the others were convicted of, he happened to be acquitted.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: That's right.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: And that was all before --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: -- he was asked to do this and I assume we can infer that he was apparently -- he was very familiar with the records of his company in the trial in which he was acquitted.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: The same time the Government had one attempt, one chance to find out what these records were and what were necessary to the trial, the second trial. And yet, we come along the second trial and they have yet -- they're unable to specify exactly what they want and they engaged in this sort of a fishing expedition to bring in all these categories without specifying at that point what it is they need for this second trial. It seems to --

turns.name: FELIX FRANKFURTER
turns.text: Then, we have to find out what kind of a lawyer he was on both sides.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: I think that's a fair conclusion.

turns.name: HAROLD BURTON
turns.text: Mr. Gressman, I just want to make sure of your answer to Justice Harlan. You said after these books were presented to the Court and the FBI men testified about them, didn't it appear that some of these records did relate to used slot machine? Was -- wasn't that clear in the testimony there were some interest in them?

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: Of the FBI agents you mean?

turns.name: HAROLD BURTON
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: Well, it's quite true, Your Honor, but there is no evidence that what the basis of what that FBI agent was stating were the items which were specified in this order to show cause in the third specification particular.

turns.name: HAROLD BURTON
turns.text: They were in these books though, weren't they?

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: Well, we don't know. He didn't set forth in his testimony what was the basis. He was testifying from general summaries he had made from this entire mass of documents and without specifying what particular items he based his conclusions on as to slot machines and other items.

turns.name: HAROLD BURTON
turns.text: But he did find a substantial number of used sales, sales of used machines.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: From some unidentified source, Your Honor, yes, as far as this record shows. I -- I assume -- I -- of course, there are other -- some other matters in my briefs which I know Your Honors --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: EUGENE GRESSMAN
turns.text: -- will read and consider this case.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Fully considered in discretion.
